funder-shortlist.question: Which funders should we apply to now?
funder-shortlist.blindSpot: Fit is scored on tracked money only — a funder with a quiet portfolio scores low on data, not on interest. Zero fit means unranked, not unfit.

templates.default: The money graph puts {top} closest to funders already backing orgs shaped like {name} — #1 of {n} embeddable funders.
templates.self: {seat} is itself on the structural shortlist — #{rank} of {n} funders with tracked money edges.
templates.isolated: {seat} has no tracked money edge — the recommender has no row to embed it with.

default.sentence: Read as a recommender, the money graph makes Coefficient Giving the #1 structural fit for an AoC-shaped org — Survival & Flourishing Fund and NSF follow, and only 12 of the 25 funders with tracked money edges carry enough tracked signal to rank at all.

rows.evidence: 13/50 edges · $204M · Center for Security and Emerging Technology, RAND Corporation
rows.flag: 1 lane amount undisclosed

rows.flag: 1 lane amount undisclosed

rows.evidence: none in lane (its one tracked edge is elsewhere)
rows.flag: only 1 tracked edge — thin structure

rivals-money.question: Who funds our rivals?
rivals-money.blindSpot: The join sees tracked positions only — absence means untracked, not unconflicted. Undisclosed stakes are exactly what this can miss.

templates.default: {name} has backed {rivals} — likely conflicted for our equity, appetite for the category proven.
templates.self: {seat} has backed {rivals} — ask about conflicts before pitching; the appetite is real.
templates.isolated: {seat} holds no tracked money edge — its rival exposure is invisible on this map.

default.sentence: 8 tracked checkbooks have already funded a flagged rival — $98.65M of it verified — while 13 funders back agent-security or evals work with no rival tie on record.

callouts.text: #1 clean target · $204M in lane, no tracked rival tie
#2 clean target · $75M in lane, no tracked rival tie

warm-routes.question: Who can introduce us to a funder?
warm-routes.blindSpot: Every hop is a sourced edge, but recall is partial — the warmest real-world intro may be unmapped. Mapped routes are upper bounds.

templates.default: The warmest mapped route to {target} starts at {from} — {hops}.
templates.self: {seat} is on the warm-route map itself — money paths open through it.
templates.isolated: {seat} has no mapped funding ties — no warm route reaches it yet.

default.sentence: Coefficient Giving — the #1 shortlist funder — is one handshake from Apollo Research; the nearest mapped doorkeeper, Nick Fitz at Juniper Ventures, sits 2 handshakes out.

callouts.text: nearest named doorkeeper · General Partner, Juniper Ventures
route hub · on 8 of 24 funding routes

rows.label: Sequoia Capital
rows.flag: no grant/investment path from any AoC-linked grantee

rows.label: Insight Partners
rows.flag: no grant/investment path from any AoC-linked grantee

within-reach.question: Which funders are within reach?
within-reach.blindSpot: Reach means mapped-path reach — funders with quiet portfolios leave no path to find. The other 46 aren't out of reach; they're off the record.

templates.default: The walk from {seat} lands on {top} first — {n} of {nf} tracked funders have a mapped money path at all.
templates.self: {seat} is #{rank} within reach — {hops} from our corner of the money graph.
templates.isolated: {seat} sits in a pocket of the money graph no walk from our corner reaches.

default.sentence: Random walks from AoC's three entry grantees find mapped paths to just 16 of the 62 tracked funders — the walk lands on Juniper Ventures first, one handshake out.

funding-bridges.question: Who gatekeeps the money?
funding-bridges.blindSpot: Doorkeeping is computed on named staff at 39 of 62 funders — the biggest tracked checkbook has no named holder on the map yet. Bridges inflate where edges are missing.

templates.default: {name} sits on {seat}'s mapped routes to {doors} of the tracked money doors.
templates.self: {seat} is itself a doorkeeper — the door to {funder} opens with it.
templates.isolated: {seat} has no path to a tracked money door yet.

default.sentence: 12 named people hold the doors to $159.8M a year of verified field money — and among doors with a name on them, only NSF's 4 program officers hold one that both pays and bridges money territories.

callouts.text: biggest door held · $100.0M/yr at Laude Institute
top people-held bridge on the map · Juniper Ventures
the only named-staff door that pays AND bridges
